analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 1 (counterparty IDs): merged `Prosperity4Data/ROUND_4/trades_round_4_day_{1,2,3}.csv` with `prices_round_4_day_*.csv` on (day,timestamp,symbol). Forward horizon K in {5,20,100} = K steps along the ordered timestamp series per (day,symbol). Aggressor: trade price vs same-tick bid1/ask1 (buy_agg if price>=ask1, sell_agg if price<=bid1). Stratification: symbol, U-spread bin (tight <=6 vs wide), burst detection (>=3 trades same day+timestamp). Graph: buyer→seller counts and 2-hop motifs. Baseline: cell mean fwd_mid_20 by (buyer,seller,symbol); residuals near zero for dominant pairs.
used_to_decide: Phase 1 gate object appended as `round4_phase1_complete` in this same file (next array element). Ran `trader_v0.py` round 4 backtests (worse+all, PnL 0) for auditable logs. Do not start Phase 2 depth until reviewed; Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300 K=20 means **flip across days**; burst−control extract test **negative** on average.

phase1_bullets.conclusion: Several Marks show **positive mean same-symbol forward mid** after their prints on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** (e.g. Mark 67 `U_buy_agg` K=5 n≈164 mean≈1.96 ticks, t≈13; Mark 22/49 passive-sell roles on U with n≈90–104 and K∈{5,20}). Voucher-specific aggressive rows are smaller n and weaker t-stats; need execution filter before claiming tradeable PnL.

Residuals of fwd_mid_20 after subtracting (buyer,seller,symbol) cell means are **~0** for high-volume pairs (Mark67→Mark22/49 on U, Mark14↔38 hydro/VEV_4000); **no** large stable second-order anomaly in this linear cell baseline.

Hub structure matches `r4_initial_analysis_summary.txt`: **Mark01→Mark22** (1339 prints) and **Mark14↔Mark38** hydro duopoly dominate; 2-hop listing is heuristic but shows Mark01→Mark22→* as frequent scaffold.

Multi-symbol bursts exist (orchestrator table). Matched **random single-trade** control: mean(**burst_fwd_U − control_fwd_U**) is **negative** at K=5 and K=20 (n≈318 bursts) — **no** evidence bursts predict **better** extract short-horizon mids than random timestamps on this crude control.
Population-level forward mids after `buy_agg` vs `sell_agg` pooled across all symbols are small; use per-symbol tables in full CSV for MM design — not a single smoking gun here.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: After **Mark 67** **buyer-aggressive** prints on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT**, pooled forward extract mid rises ~**2 ticks** at K=5 and ~**1.8** at K=20 (n≈164; t≈5.9–13).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: Tape-only; **worse** fills not tested; role is aggressive **buyer** — any live rule must respect limits and adverse selection at touch.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: **Mark 22** as **passive seller** on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** rows: positive mean fwd mid at K=5 and K=20 (n=90; t≈10–4.7).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: Passive seller → upward drift may reflect **liquidity provision** context; needs pairing with aggressor identity and gate (Phase 3 Sonic).

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: **Mark 49** passive seller on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** shows similar positive short-horizon fwd (n≈104).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: Smaller actor than Mark22; confirm not duplicate of same micro episodes.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: **Mark 14** passive seller on **VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT** at **K=100** weak positive mean (~1.1 ticks, n=328, t≈2.2).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: Long horizon signal is mild; overlapping windows not de-duplicated.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.edge: Directed pair **Mark01→Mark22** is the structural spine for voucher basket flow (1339 rows) — candidate **scheduler** feature for any conditional model even if mean reversion on VEV_5300 alone is weak.
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates.caveat: This is **structural** not yet a signed markout edge; per-symbol Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300 K=20 means **flip sign across days** (see stability CSV).

no_edge_or_negative_findings: **Mark01→Mark22** on **VEV_5300** fwd_mid_20: day means **-0.70 / +0.18 / +0.014** (n 32/31/69) — **not stable** across days for a simple fade/trend rule.
**Burst** timestamps do **not** outperform **random** single-trade timestamps on extract forward mid (mean burst−control < 0 for K=5,20).
**(buyer,seller,symbol)** cell-mean baseline for fwd_mid_20 leaves **near-zero** residuals for dominant hydro and U pairs — no obvious mis-modeled bot residual edge at this granularity.

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 2 (`round4work/suggested direction.txt` Phase 2): (1) Named-bot + **burst B** = same (day,timestamp) with Mark01→Mark22 and ≥3 distinct VEV symbols — forward **VEV_5300** mid vs all other trades. (2) Microstructure on **U**: microprice deviation vs mid, spread vs |fwd|, spread compression (Δspr over 20 steps within day). (3) Lead–lag: per-timestamp aggressive signed flow on U vs same-timestamp **VEV_5300** fwd 20 + 1-row lag. (4) Regime split: Mark67 buy_agg fwd U@20 under tight U spread (≤6) vs wide (none in sample). (5) Sketch IV smile residual (3 strikes, coarse T) vs Mark01 same timestamp. (6) Pair markouts **VEV_5300** fwd5 for top pairs (only M01→M22 had rows).
used_to_decide: Phase 2 gate below. **Burst B** refines Phase 1 (random-control burst null on *extract*): here **VEV_5300** fwd means are **higher** inside basket bursts than outside (n≈1464 vs 2817), a **orthogonal** voucher-local effect. Phase 1 Mark67 U signal is **not** contradicted but is **tight-book only** in this slice (all 164 buy_agg rows have U spread ≤6). Microprice/spread compression correlations with future |U move| are ~0. IV sketch vs Mark01 same-ts is null (tiny n when Mark01 prints align with subsampled IV rows).

phase1_interaction: Phase-1 **Mark67 buy_agg on U** mean fwd@20 (~+1.81) is **consistent** with Phase-2 regime table: **all** n=164 such prints occur when **U spread ≤6** (wide bucket empty). Phase-1 **burst vs random extract** negative result is **orthogonal** to Phase-2 finding that **Mark01→Mark22 multi-VEV bursts** associate with **higher** short-horizon **VEV_5300** mid drift vs non-burst prints. Phase-1 **Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300** pooled negative fwd5 (≈−0.15) is **compatible** with burst conditioning: bursts lift mean fwd but most M01→M22 prints are not in the burst-B subset.

phase2_bullets.conclusion: Burst-B rows show **higher** mean **VEV_5300** forward mid at K=5 and K=20 than non-burst trades (mean ≈+0.20/+0.31 vs +0.007/−0.002; frac_pos higher). Mean-revert vs trend split (sign(fwd5)·sign(fwd20)) still mixed counts — not a clean one-sided MR rule.
Correlations of microprice deviation and spread compression with future |U mid move| are **near zero** (≈−0.002 to ∐.02) on ~30k ticks — **no** strong linear microprice edge in this pass.

phase2_bullets.bullet: 3) Cross-instrument lead–lag (U aggressive flow vs VEV_5300 fwd20)
phase2_bullets.conclusion: Same-timestamp correlation **+≈0.03–0.06** per day (weak positive); 1-row lag similar magnitude — **not** a standalone high-conviction lead–lag edge.
**Confirms** Phase-1 Mark67 pattern is **only** observable under **tight U** books in this sample (wide bucket n=0).
Coarse 3-strike IV smile residual vs Mark01 print at same timestamp: **no** material mean shift (n for m01=1 is tiny).

**Mark22** as seller on **VEV_5300**: mean fwd5 **≈−0.19** (n=163, frac_pos≈0.25). **Mark01→Mark22** on **VEV_5300** fwd5 mean **≈−0.15** (n=132) — **avoid passive liquidity** that leans against this side without offsetting logic.
Not simulated (no own-inventory path in tape analysis); deferred to trader design once execution model exists.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.edge: **Burst-B** (Mark01→Mark22 multi-VEV same timestamp) → **higher VEV_5300** short-horizon fwd mid vs baseline trades (K=5/20; n≈1464 burst rows).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.caveat: Still tape-mid only; **worse** fills not tested; define execution (join vs lift) before sizing.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.edge: **Mark67** aggressive buys on **U** under **tight U spread** retain Phase-1 positive fwd@20 (≈+1.81, n=164) — Phase-2 regime table shows **no** wide-spread observations in sample.
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.caveat: Conditional on spread≤6 only; cannot extrapolate to wide books.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.edge: Weak same-timestamp positive link: **U aggressive signed flow** vs **VEV_5300** fwd20 (corr ~0.03–0.06/day).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.caveat: Magnitude small; likely not tradable after costs.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.edge: **Do-not-quote / fade** pressure: **Mark22** selling **VEV_5300** prints followed by **negative** mean fwd5 (≈−0.19).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.caveat: Execution rule must specify passive vs aggressive stance; markout is mid-based.

top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.edge: **Mark01→Mark22** on **VEV_5300** short fwd5 is negative on average (≈−0.15) — complements burst-B (bursts are subset; net pair drift can still be negative).
top_5_tradeable_edge_candidates_phase2.caveat: Phase-1 day-stability for K=20 was poor; Phase-2 adds burst conditioning as refinement.

phase2_negative_or_null: U **microprice** deviation vs mid and **spread compression** show ~**zero** linear predictive power for future |U move|.
IV smile residual vs Mark01 same timestamp (sketch) — **null** with tiny aligned n.
Inventory-aware MM not analyzed on tape (no sim).

analysis_description: Round 4 Phase 3 (Sonic + inclineGod on Round 4): **inner-join** aligned panel per day on `timestamp` for VEV_5200, VEV_5300, VELVETFRUIT_EXTRACT (+ HYDROGEL for spread correlations), same as R3 `analyze_vev_5200_5300_tight_gate_r3.py`. `tight` = s5200≤2 and s5300≤2. Recomputed **K=20** forward extract mid on panel; **Welch** tight vs loose by day and pooled. **inclineGod:** corr(s5200,s5300), spreads vs U, vs hydro. **Interaction:** Mark01→Mark22 **VEV_5300** trades all fall when `sonic_tight` (n=132 gate_on, 0 gate_off); fwd@20 still **negative** pooled (≈−0.12) but **day-stable** pattern matches Phase-1 table. **Burst-B × gate:** most burst-B VEV_5300 rows are `sonic_tight`; mean fwd in that cell still **negative** (≈−0.13, n=162). **Mark67** U buy_agg: **higher** mean fwd@20 when **gate on** (2.06 vs 1.77; n=27 vs 137).
used_to_decide: Phase 3 gate object follows. **Sonic claim validates on Round 4 extract** (pooled Welch t≈7.9, p≈2e-15). **Counterparty × gate:** Mark67 U signal **strengthens when joint tight** (interaction table). **Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300** prints in this sample are **100% under joint tight** yet **mean fwd@20 stays negative** — gate **does not** flip that pair edge to positive (refines Phase 2 burst-B story: burst lifts unconditional VEV_5300 fwd but **Mark01→Mark22** subset is still a bad drift at K=20).

comparison_to_phase1_without_gate: Phase-1 pooled **Mark01→Mark22 VEV_5300** K=20 day table is unchanged in sign pattern; Phase-3 shows those prints occur **only** under Sonic tight in this tape slice, yet **mean fwd@20 remains negative** — so the joint gate **cleans the population** (all basket prints are tight-surface times) but **does not** by itself create a **positive** M01→M22 markout at this horizon.
comparison_to_phase2_without_gate: Phase-2 **Burst-B** raised unconditional **VEV_5300** fwd vs non-burst; Phase-3 cross shows **Burst-B ∩ tight** still has **negative** mean fwd@20 for **VEV_5300** rows (n=162). So **burst** and **tight** are **not sufficient** for a long-5300-after-basket rule at K=20; execution / other filters still needed.

phase3_tier_a_candidates.note: Tier-A for **interaction** with Sonic gate; still mid-only.

phase3_tier_a_candidates.pattern: Sonic tight vs loose on **extract** fwd@20 (panel)
phase3_tier_a_candidates.effect: Pooled mean tight **+0.32** vs loose **−0.12**; Welch t≈7.9 (p≈2e-15).
phase3_tier_a_candidates.note: Confirms Sonic community finding on **Round 4** mid tape.
Wing spreads move together; cross-asset spread corr to U is weak in this join.

phase3_negative_or_null: **Mark01→Mark22** on **VEV_5300** at K=20: **all** n=132 prints have `sonic_tight=True` but **mean fwd≈−0.12** — gate does not rescue this counterparty leg.
**Burst-B ∩ tight** on **VEV_5300** rows: mean fwd@20 still **≈−0.13** (n=162) — contradicts a naive ‘always long 5300 after basket in tight surface’ at this horizon.

analysis_description: Round 4 **execution falsification** of naive Sonic-gate extract long: `trader_v3.py` buys **U** at ask on **rising** joint-tight edge and sells at bid on falling edge. Backtest sums final-timestamp **profit_and_loss** across products from saved JSON activity log (same as printed **Total profit**).
used_to_decide: Strategy **fails badly**: total PnL **-291,883** (worse) / **-291,967** (all) over Round 4 days 1–3; **~8.4k** SUBMISSION fill rows on **U**. Confirms community caveat: **mid** forward edge under the gate does **not** translate to paying spread on **unconditional** aggressive long at gate turn-on. Next: passive / sized / counterparty-conditioned entries; note `TestRunner` calls `run()` **before** `OrderMatchMaker` populates `market_trades`, so same-tick counterparty gating from `state.market_trades` inside `run()` is unreliable without upstream change.

analysis_description: Round 4 **passive U** under Sonic + tight U: `trader_v4.py` uses EMA(mid) dip/profit bands while joint gate on; improved bid (`min(bid+1,ask-1)` when spread≥2) so `--match-trades worse` can match tape sells; flatten when gate opens.
used_to_decide: PnL **-468** worse / **-444** all (still negative but **~600×** smaller loss than v3); **165** worse SUBMISSION rows vs **8426** in v3 — spread cost dominated v3. Remaining drag: exit still pays spread (ask sells); Mark67 interaction not yet wired (needs tape at `run()` or runner reorder). Next iteration can try **only** passive round-trip (improved bid / improved ask) or smaller `EXIT_Q` / wider `PROFIT`.

analysis_description: **Infrastructure:** `TestRunner` now calls `__attach_raw_market_trades` so `state.market_trades` holds **this timestamp’s** tape **before** `Trader.run()` (Round 4 counterparty fields usable). **Execution:** `trader_v5.py` — Mark **67** **buy-aggressive** on **U** (`price>=ask1`) gated on **Sonic** (VEV_5200/5300 spread≤2) + U spread≤6; spread==1 uses small **ask** lift (else improved bid under worse-match). **Bugfix:** avoid `isinstance(tr, Trade)` in the trader module (duplicate `Trade` class under different import paths silently dropped all tape rows).
used_to_decide: Rare overlap: only **27** timestamps across days 1–3 satisfy Mark67 buy_agg on U **and** Sonic gate (see `r5_m67_*_counts.txt`). Backtest **v5** PnL **-42** (worse=all identical here): **22** SUBMISSION rows, **66** abs fill qty on U — small edge after execution still **negative** at current exit (EMA+ask) and sizing. Mid-only Phase-3 lift for Mark67×gate does **not** yet yield positive PnL in this discrete rule; next: passive-only exits, tune `ASK_LIFT_Q`/`PROFIT`, or add VEV leg per burst-B (separate risk).
